text: Chapter 16 presents three interconnected techniques for advanced harmonic development: reharmonizing modal progressions as hybrid chords, using modal root motion to drive constant structure patterns, and anchoring ambiguous harmonies with pedal tones or ostinatos. Effective modal progressions require careful melody selection—the melody must be diatonic to the chosen key and ideally resolve to the modal root to reinforce tonality. Hybrid chords gain textural power when deployed in series rather than in isolation, creating an impressionistic effect that develops the progression beyond traditional voicings. Pedal tones and ostinatos serve a crucial grounding function, establishing tonal clarity even when the underlying harmonic movement is ambiguous. To ensure listener comprehension, modal phrases and constant structure patterns require repetition, often with reinforcement from tonic pedals or ostinatos that underscore the modal quality. This integration of modal patterns, hybrid voicings, and constant structures—balanced with judicious repetition and textural consistency—offers a comprehensive toolkit for contemporary harmonic reharmonization.
